content.full_text:
November 8, 1994


Gregory Taylor
Metropolitan Water District
of Southern California
350 South Grand Avenue
Los Angeles, CA  90071

                                        Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
                                             Our File No. I-94-289a

Dear Mr. Taylor:

     You previously requested informal assistance concerning new
"ethics" provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ which
further limit the receipt of gifts and honoraria by specified
public officials effective January 1, 1995.

     The purpose of this letter is to inform you that, in addition
to Assembly Bill 1542 (Ch. 36, Stats. 1994), Assembly Bill 3126
(Ch. 1105, Stats. 1994) also amended existing Commission laws
concerning gifts and honoraria.  This new law will also be
effective January 1, 1995.  Assembly Bill 3126 will, in addition
to other changes, subject "candidates" for local office, including
special districts, to the ban on the receipt of honoraria and the
gift limit applicable to elected state offices.

     The new law does not generally change the conclusions in the
advice letter previously sent to you, Our File No. I-94-289.
However, Commission staff will be reviewing this new legislation
to also determine whether amendments to existing regulations are
necessary with respect to candidates.


_________________________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000 - 18954.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA  95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-94-289a
Page 2


Feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901, should you have
any further questions regarding this matter.2

Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Luisa Menchaca
Counsel, Legal Division




2 Copies of Commission regulations and opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question_synthetic: How do new amendments to the Political Reform Act (Assembly Bills 1542 and 3126, effective January 1, 1995) affect restrictions on gifts and honoraria for public officials and candidates, particularly regarding candidates for local office and special districts?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Assembly Bill 3126 amends existing law to subject candidates for local office, including special districts, to the ban on receipt of honoraria and gift limits applicable to elected state officers, effective January 1, 1995. The new law does not materially change prior advice conclusions, though FPPC staff will review whether regulatory amendments are necessary.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a follow-up/supplemental advice letter (I-94-289a) responding to a prior request. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are present; content is conveyed in body paragraphs. References a prior advice letter (I-94-289). Document focuses on legislative changes rather than specific factual scenarios.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How do new amendments to the Political Reform Act (Assembly Bills 1542 and 3126, effective January 1, 1995) affect restrictions on gifts and honoraria for public officials and candidates, particularly regarding candidates for local office and special districts?

CONCLUSION: Assembly Bill 3126 amends existing law to subject candidates for local office, including special districts, to the ban on receipt of honoraria and gift limits applicable to elected state officers, effective January 1, 1995. The new law does not materially change prior advice conclusions, though FPPC staff will review whether regulatory amendments are necessary.

embedding.summary: This FPPC informal advice letter informs the Metropolitan Water District that Assembly Bill 3126, effective January 1, 1995, extends restrictions on gifts and honoraria to candidates for local office and special districts. The letter clarifies that this new legislation does not substantially alter conclusions from a previous advice letter on the same topic.
